sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Left knee pain and high blood pressure.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports ongoing left knee pain, with some erythema and edema, and decreased range of motion. He reports a prior rotator cuff repair two years ago. He denies fever, chills, nausea, or vomiting. He takes norvasc 5 mg daily for hypertension, which he monitors at home. He reports no pins and needles in his feet or swelling in his legs. He has been taking his medication consistently and reports no elevation in blood pressure.
Findings
The patient's blood pressure is elevated today. White blood cell count is normal. The left knee demonstrates mild erythema, edema, and a small effusion. Heart rhythm is regular and rate is appropriate. X-ray of the left knee is normal, suggesting a mild strain.
Assessment
The patient presents with left knee pain, likely secondary to a mild strain, and elevated blood pressure.
Plan
Order an autoimmune profile and a lipid profile. Start meloxicam 15 mg once daily for pain management. Refer for physical therapy. Continue metformin 500 mg twice daily for diabetes management. Send a report in the next two weeks with blood pressure readings.
